notes: Arturia Wurli V is a physical model of the Wurlitzer electric piano, capturing the instrument's characteristic reed-and-pickup mechanism from first principles rather than sample playback. Physical modelling allows the Wurli V to respond to velocity, touch, and performance nuance in ways that closely mirror the original hardware — including the mechanical noise of reed attack, pickup crosstalk between notes, and the subtle variation between individual reeds. The Wurlitzer's distinctive slightly overdriven, slightly buzzy midrange character is shaped by reed bite, pickup settings, and the built-in tremolo effect. Overdrive and tremolo automation are central to the instrument's expressive range, from clean R&B comping to heavily driven rock and soul leads.

Reed Bite.description: Reed character/bite — controls the physical interaction between reed and pickup; higher values increase the buzzy, aggressive midrange character

Pickup Crosstalk.description: Pickup crosstalk — simulates electromagnetic interference between adjacent note pickups; adds subtle harmonic complexity

Tremolo Rate.description: Tremolo rate — speed of the amplitude tremolo effect; integral to the classic Wurlitzer soul/R&B sound

Tremolo Depth.description: Tremolo depth — intensity of the tremolo amplitude modulation

Overdrive.description: Overdrive amount — from clean sparkle to the heavily saturated drive used on classic rock recordings

Release Noise.description: Release noise level — the mechanical thunk of keys releasing; adds realism to the physical model

Brightness.description: Brightness/tone — overall high-frequency content; controls the pick-up EQ character

Reverb.description: Reverb send level — room or plate reverb adds space to the direct electric piano sound

Chorus.description: Chorus depth — adds subtle ensemble width
